title: AI-Powered Social Engineering: What Executives Need to Know
excerpt: Generative AI has eliminated the barriers that previously filtered out unsophisticated attackers. The resulting threat is more personal, more convincing, and more difficult to detect than anything that came before it.

title: The Executive Home Network: An Overlooked Attack Surface
excerpt: Corporate security programs protect corporate infrastructure. The executive's home, where most real decisions are made and most sensitive communications happen, falls entirely outside that perimeter.

title: Travel Security for High-Profile Individuals
excerpt: Travel compresses risk into a predictable, observable window. Adversaries know your location, your schedule, and your network context. Most travelers do not know theirs are being watched.

title: The First 60 Minutes: Incident Response Decision Framework
excerpt: The decisions made in the first hour of a breach determine its ultimate scope. A structured framework for the critical initial response window.

title: Credential Exposure: How Leaked Passwords Become Weapons
excerpt: Dark web credential markets are sophisticated, fast-moving, and actively targeting executive accounts. Understanding the pipeline from breach to attack.

title: Supply Chain Blind Spots: Third-Party Risk in the Enterprise
excerpt: Your vendors are your perimeter extensions. An analysis of how supply chain risk has evolved and what organizations are consistently missing.

title: Why Most Security Awareness Programs Fail and What Works Instead
excerpt: Annual compliance training does not change behavior. Evidence-based approaches to building genuine security awareness in organizations.
